commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A maintainer should explicitly accept or reject the authenticated ChatGPT file-download boundary; the final head has no narrow automated repair to perform.; Cleared: The dedicated pass found no concrete remaining security defect: final-head code restricts cookie-bearing requests to known HTTPS ChatGPT file endpoints and strips cookies when redirects leave that boundary. (sha=ac32c892437ad4b92f0e1ddb5d9e90ad195e38a9)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds ChatGPT browser file-artifact discovery and persistence, constrained authenticated downloads with scoped button recovery, session metadata and archive gating, documentation, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body gives a high-confidence authenticated current-main reproduction where ChatGPT exposed a real CSV control but Oracle persisted only `transcript.md`, followed by a final-branch run that saved the CSV and transcript.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should explicitly accept or reject the authenticated ChatGPT file-download boundary; the final head has no narrow automated repair to perform.

target.title: [codex] save ChatGPT browser file artifacts
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the narrow file-artifact boundary proposed in steipete/oracle#244:

- add `kind: "file"` session artifacts for ChatGPT browser runs
- discover downloadable file anchors from the assistant response and captured answer text/transcript
- restrict authenticated ChatGPT downloads to known file artifact endpoints only:
  - `/backend-api/files/<id>/download|content`
  - `/backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_*`
  - `/backend-api/sandbox/download?path=/mnt/data/...`
- reject non-file ChatGPT backend APIs such as `/backend-api/me` and conversation endpoints before sending cookies
- recover scoped ChatGPT behavior buttons such as `Download the CSV` when a detected current-turn file candidate cannot be fetched directly
- avoid download-button polling when no file candidate exists, and do not click global/stale page-level download buttons
- include saved files in session artifact metadata and keep the archive guard from archiving before required artifacts are saved

This intentionally does not fetch arbitrary external links or arbitrary ChatGPT `/backend-api/*` URLs from the answer.

## Why

After #169, browser sessions persist transcripts, Deep Research reports, and generated images, but generic ChatGPT-generated files such as ZIPs, wheels, source distributions, CSVs, or PDFs were still stranded in the live ChatGPT conversation. This extends the same artifact layer without adding a new public API.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/browser/artifacts.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptFiles.test.ts tests/cli/sessionDisplay.test.ts tests/cli/sessionRunner.test.ts tests/sessionManager.test.ts`
  - 97 tests passed
- `pnpm run check`
- `pnpm run build`
- `pnpm run test`
  - 1,090 passed, 41 skipped
- `pnpm run test:packed-cli`
- `git diff --check`
- branch autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Live proof status

Current `main` reproduced #244 in authenticated browser session `pr245-main-file-repro-sync`: ChatGPT generated a real CSV and exposed a live assistant download control, but Oracle saved only `transcript.md`.

The updated branch then passed a fresh authenticated end-to-end run in session `pr245-maintainer-file-e2e-final`. The sandbox URL returned 404, the scoped current-run assistant button fallback clicked exactly one matching control, and Oracle saved both the generated file and transcript:

```text
[browser] Found 1 downloadable file link(s) in captured answer text.
[browser] Found 1 downloadable file candidate(s).
[browser] Failed to save downloadable file 1/1: download failed: 404 Not Found
[browser] Clicked 1 assistant download button(s).
[browser] Saved 1 downloadable file artifact(s).
[browser] Saved transcript artifact
```

Saved artifact, with account/conversation data omitted:

```text
oracle_pr245_e2e_final.csv
text/csv, 29 bytes

name,value
alpha,1
beta,2
```

Session metadata and rendered output list the CSV as a `file` artifact plus `transcript.md`; the transcript includes an `Artifacts` reference to the CSV.

Fresh no-file browser proof in session `pr245-maintainer-no-file-final` completed in 11.2 seconds with only `transcript.md` and no download fallback polling or auto-save failure log.

`Fixes #244`

## Notes

- The archive guard now requires detected browser file artifacts to be saved before auto-archiving, matching the existing generated-image safety model.
- Regression tests reject `/backend-api/me`, conversation endpoints, unsafe sandbox paths, non-`file_` estuary IDs, non-default ports, external cookie forwarding, no-candidate polling, global/stale button scans, duplicate DOM/text references, and unsafe broad fallback after partial direct saves.
target.author: pdurlej
